evidence.scientific_clarity: Stacked bars sum to 1 across 5 conditions with 4 classes, y-axis 0-100% with percent labels — fully matches brief.
evidence.typography: Default DejaVu Sans used (no fonttype=42 set); font sizes 9-11pt are reasonable but the default font is not publication-quality.
evidence.palette: Green (#55A868) and red (#C44E52) assigned to categorical classes B and C — violates directional color discipline; palette is otherwise CB-safe.
evidence.layout_density: 6x4 figure with tight_layout, 0.6 bar width, clean spines removed; space is well-used with no gaping whitespace or overlapping labels.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption explicitly names green and red for categorical classes, which a reviewer will flag; legend is upper-right and may overlap bars depending on data.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF embeds DejaVuSans as Type 3 (bitmap glyphs, not selectable text); script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (12K), figure.png (26K), figure.svg (30K), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption describes the chart structure and color mapping but contains no numerical finding, trend statement, or specific claim about the data.

overall_strengths: Clean layout with removed top/right spines, tight_layout, and appropriate axis formatting (percent labels, labeled axes).
All required output files are present and non-trivial; SVG bonus output included.
overall_weaknesses: PDF uses Type 3 (bitmap) fonts — no pdf.fonttype=42 — making text non-selectable and unsuitable for journal submission.
Green and red assigned to categorical classes violates directional color discipline, and the caption lacks any quantitative finding.
